regions.text: Time and Space are connected. Astronomers use light-years to measure the vast distances to the stars.
(Diagram: The moon and stars in the night sky). A light-year is the distance light travels in one year.
Modern telescopes see stars billions of light-years away. Because light takes time to reach us, we see them as they were in the past.
(Illustrations: A large telescope and an observatory). astronomer: star scientist. astronomy: study of stars. FUTURE: will study. PRESENT: study(ies). PAST: studied.
